channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Launches Strikes on Iran; Tehran Claims Attacks on U.S. Fleet
item.description: The exchange came after President Trump said Iranian forces had shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: A Challenge in the U.S.-Iran Talks: Both Sides Demand Victory
item.description: Washington and Tehran would need to defend any potential deal as a win for their side. And each has a leader whose approach to talks is vexing mediators.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in Tehran on Monday after Iran and Israel exchanged fire.

item.title: Infantino’s Yearslong Effort to Woo Trump for the World Cup
item.description: Gianni Infantino, the FIFA president, has unabashedly courted the president’s favor. Soccer officials privately ask, who really benefits?
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Rebecca R. Ruiz
item.media:description: President Trump with Gianni Infantino, FIFA’s president, at the White House in 2019.

item.title: Drones Stray Into Neighboring Countries as Russia and Ukraine Battle
item.description: Drones launched by both Russia and Ukraine are veering off course, menacing countries that are not at war and driving their citizens to seek shelter.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:credit: Andrej Vasilenko for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ligita Visockiene, the principal of a primary school in Vilnius, Lithuania, in a room the school uses as a shelter. The school has detailed plans for what to do in the event of attack but, she added, “We never thought we would have to actually use the plan.”

item.title: The Roar of ‘Cease-Fire’ in a Once-Thriving City
item.description: The city of Nabatieh once spoke to the changing fortunes of Lebanon. Now, amid unending war, it speaks to the country’s worst fears.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and Daniel Berehulak
item.media:description: An empty road to Nabatieh, in southern Lebanon, last week.

item.title: Why a U.K. By-Election in Makerfield Could Topple Prime Minister Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, is running for a seat in Parliament. It’s a vital step in a campaign to oust his party colleague Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Andy Burnham launching his special election campaign last month. He was a member of Parliament for more than a decade before becoming mayor of Greater Manchester.

item.title: One of Pope Leo’s Best Friends Works in Spain. They Talk About Cars.
item.description: Pope Leo and Armando Jesús Lovera have known each other for decades. They have watched World Cup games together, gone on road trips and once searched for a teddy bear for Mr. Lovera’s future wife.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:credit: Gianfranco Tripodo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armando Jesús Lovera in Valladolid, Spain, at the local headquarters of the Augustinian order.

item.title: Somali Referee Says His World Cup Dream Is Dashed After U.S. Denies Entry
item.description: “I had the right papers and everything,” Omar Abdulkadir Artan said in his first interview since he was turned back. He would have been the first Somali to referee a game in the tournament.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.media:description: Omar Abdulkadir Artan, right, during an Africa Cup of Nations match in Morocco last year.

item.title: Many Feared Trapped Under Earthquake Rubble in Philippines
item.description: Families hope to recover their loved ones after the most powerful earthquake in 50 years to hit the Philippines.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See
item.media:description: An earthquake caused extensive damage to the city of General Santos, in the southern Philippines, and killed at least 38 people.

item.title: Man Arrested in ‘Brutal’ Stabbing in Belfast, Police Say
item.description: The suspect, who the police said is 30 and Sudanese, was charged with having attacked another man in an assault in Belfast that was recorded and spread quickly online.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: Police forensic markers at the scene of a stabbing in Belfast, Northern Ireland, on Tuesday.

item.title: Germany Drops Plan to Build Warplane With Allies, Hindering Push to Rearm
item.description: Berlin pulled out of the Future Combat Air System, a project with Spain and France that had been seen as central to European efforts to face down Russia and reduce reliance on the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:credit: Geoffroy Van Der Hasselt/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A model of a fighter that was to be part of the Future Combat Air System in France in 2023. The project was announced almost a decade ago.

item.title: A ‘Miraculous Transformation’: How Kim Jong-un Fortified North Korea
item.description: He used the pandemic to ruthlessly tighten his grip on the country. Then he energized its economy by leveraging Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:description: Kim Jong-un arriving at the Dong Dang railway station in Vietnam, near the Chinese border, in 2019.

item.title: Nikol Pashinyan Wins Re-election in Armenia
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan overcame a pressure campaign by Moscow and was on track to win a mandate to move ahead on peace talks that President Trump helped broker.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.media:description: Nikol Pashinyan at a polling station in Yerevan, Armenia, on Sunday.

item.title: The 15-Hour Fight With Iran Showed the Bind Israel Is In
item.description: As it braces for a possible U.S.-Iranian peace deal, Israel now knows that if it responds forcefully to attacks by Hezbollah, Iran may strike Israel with missiles.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in March.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

item.title: Trump’s Sharp Turn on China: Embracing It as a Peer Power
item.description: President Trump’s warming with Xi Jinping of China, a leader he admires, has ignited anxieties in Washington and across Asia.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: The U.S. and Chinese governments rolled out a new phrase, “constructive strategic stability,” during President Trump’s meeting in Beijing last month with the leader of China, Xi Jinping.

item.title: What to Know About the Sea Drone That Rescued Downed Apache Crew
item.description: It was the first U.S. rescue carried out by an autonomous surface vessel and remotely piloted by a human operator, according to a military spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Air Canada Pilot Accused of Flying for 17 Years Without Proper License
item.description: The pilot, who retired last year before the investigation, held some valid flight credentials, officials said, but not the one required to be a captain.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman
item.media:credit: Sammy Kogan/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: An Air Canada pilot was the captain for 900 flights between 2009 and 2025 despite not having the proper license, Canadian officials said.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Trump Vows to Retaliate for Downed Helicopter
item.description: The retaliatory U.S. attacks came on Tuesday as Israeli forces pounded southern Lebanon while targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “The United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack,” President Trump said on social media before the strikes.

item.title: A.I. Politics
item.description: Much of the discussion about artificial intelligence focuses on economic disruption. But it could reshape political life, too.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Paulo Nunes dos Santos for The New York Times
item.media:description: A protest in Dublin, Ireland, last year.

item.title: Are You Traveling to the U.S. for the 2026 World Cup? We’d Like to Hear From You.
item.description: Tell us about your experience buying tickets, finding a place to stay, making travel arrangements and, if you’re coming to the U.S., dealing with entry requirements.
item.dc:creator: Danial Adkison
item.media:description: MetLife Stadium in New Jersey will be hosting eight World Cup matches.

item.title: Pope Leo Meets Bad Bunny
item.description: The two crossed paths in Spain, where they have been on decidedly different tours. Sorry, no photos were released.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:description: Bad Bunny performing at the Super Bowl in Santa Clara, Calif., in February; Pope Leo in Madrid on Tuesday.

item.title: 6 Countries Announce Sanctions Targeting Israeli Settler Networks
item.description: The measures aim to “disrupt the flows of finance” supporting attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank, said the British government, which coordinated them with France and other nations.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: An attack by settlers in October on two Palestinian brothers in Huwara, in the West Bank.

item.title: D4RLYL, a Robot Magician, Is Denied Membership in The Magic Circle
item.description: No, according to The Magic Circle, a British society that rejected a robot named D4RYL from its ranks. “Wonder is emotional, not just mechanical,” its president said.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: David Penn and D4RYL. Two magicians, or just one?

item.title: Iran’s Soccer Federation Says Its World Cup Tickets Have Been Revoked
item.description: It did not say who had interfered but blamed the United States, one of the tournament’s hosts, after months of war. FIFA said it was looking for a solution.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s players arriving on Sunday in Tijuana, Mexico, where they will be based for the World Cup.

item.title: Billionaires’ Billions Are Increasing Faster Than Ever
item.description: Elon Musk’s potential new status as a trillionaire demonstrates in real time why there has been such a rapid rise in the concentration of wealth at the top.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen

item.title: Israel Strikes Lebanese City of Tyre as U.S.-Brokered Truce Falters
item.description: The bombardment followed Israeli evacuation warning for the entire city, a day after Iran threatened to attack Israel again if it kept up its offensive against Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Netanyahu’s Bow to Trump’s Iran Pressure Spurs New Criticism
item.description: Opponents attacked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for halting strikes against Iran after a call with President Trump, saying that he was letting the United States make Israel’s decisions.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at the Knesset, Israel’s parliament, last week.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Farnaz Fassihi and Euan Ward

item.title: The Ex-Mayor of Arcadia, Her Boyfriend and the Chinese Government
item.description: Eileen Wang pleaded guilty to acting as a foreign agent. But what could Beijing want from the mayor of a small California city known as the “Chinese Beverly Hills”?
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin
item.media:description: Eileen Wang, the former mayor of Arcadia, Calif., exiting federal court after pleading guilty last month.

item.title: Crew Is Rescued After U.S. Helicopter Goes Down Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It was not immediately clear what led to the incident, which came at a time when the cease-fire in the war has grown tenuous.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: I.C.C. Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Harassment Claims
item.description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, faced an accusation of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court.

item.title: U.S. Denies Entry to World Cup Referee From Somalia
item.description: Omar Artan was one of 52 referees selected for the FIFA World Cup. But on Saturday he was denied entry because of “vetting concerns,” U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Mohamed and Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Omar Artan will not officiate games at the World Cup, FIFA said.

item.title: Iran and Israel Pull Back, After Fierce Exchange of Attacks
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, an Iranian-backed militia, is complicating efforts to end the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Ronen Bergman and Maggie Haberman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard of former ayatollahs Khomeini and Khamenei in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Kim Jong-un’s Triumph
item.description: He leveraged the war in Ukraine to become North Korea’s most powerful leader to date.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: Videos Show Chinese Businesses Hawking North Korean Labor
item.description: On social media, Chinese entrepreneurs are touting cheap labor across the border, as trade between the two countries regains momentum.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang and Choe Sang-Hun

item.title: Scientist Charged With Conspiring to Smuggle Mpox Virus Into U.S.
item.description: The virologist was stopped at the Detroit airport after working in Congo during an mpox epidemic. His lawyer said the material was for research.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Victoire Douniama for The New York Times
item.media:description: A vial to be sent for mpox testing in the Democratic Republic of Congo in 2024.

item.title: Vance Blames Migrant ‘Invasion’ for UK Stabbing
item.description: British officials accused Vice President JD Vance of trying to “stir up division” in his comments on the murder of Henry Nowak, whose killer was sentenced to life in prison last week.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Tributes for Henry Nowak outside the Portswood Police Station in Southampton, southern England, last week.

item.title: U.S. Judge Reverses Decision on Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
item.description: The judge had previously ruled that the woman had been improperly deported by the Trump administration and had ordered her returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Justin Makangara for The New York Times
item.media:description: A hotel in Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo, housing deportees from the United States in April.

item.title: Patrick Bruel, French Singer, Is Taken Into Custody Over Sex Assault Claims
item.description: Mr. Bruel, long a beloved celebrity in France, has denied allegations raised by 13 women dating to 1997.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:description: The French singer and actor Patrick Bruel in Paris last year.

item.title: Iran-Israel Escalation Ignited in Lebanon, Which Is Trapped in Their Conflict
item.description: The power struggle between Iran and Israel to define the new rules of the Middle East plays out in Lebanon, which the other two countries have used to pursue their own ends.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers on Sunday at a damaged building in a neighborhood on the southern outskirts of Beirut, a Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: China Reasserts Itself, to Contain North Korea’s Tilt Toward Russia
item.description: On a rare visit to North Korea, China’s leader, Xi Jinping, projected unity but also sought to remind Kim Jong-un that he is the senior partner in their alliance.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A giant screen in Beijing displayed news footage on Monday of the meeting between China’s president, Xi Jinping, and North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, in North Korea.

item.title: Are U.S. and Israel on the Same Page in Mideast Wars?
item.description: President Trump has voiced his frustration with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, but it is not clear how able he is to rein in Israeli military action.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has found himself on the sidelines of talks to end the war in Iran.

item.title: The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
item.description: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jes Aznar for The New York Times
item.media:description: The president of Solaric, a rooftop-solar installer based near Manila, said people were now seeing the technology as “a way to survive this new age.”

item.title: Houthis Threaten to Widen Iran War by Blocking Israeli Shipping in Red Sea
item.description: The Yemeni militia group, which is backed by Iran, said it would impose a partial blockade in the waterway, a move that would further strangle supply routes and aggravate the conflict in the Mideast.
item.dc:creator: Shuaib Almosawa and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Houthi supporters in Sanaa, Yemen, sit around an Iranian flag in April at a demonstration in solidarity with Tehran.

item.title: After new attacks, Israelis lament a ‘never-ending loop of war.’
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sheltering underground during an air raid siren in Tel Aviv on Monday.

item.title: Why Iran Risked an Attack on Israel
item.description: Iran’s leaders want to show they are serious about defending their Hezbollah allies in Lebanon and maintaining the regional balance of power, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of a projectile that landed in northern Israel, early Monday morning.

item.title: What to Know About Mahshahr, the Iranian Petrochemical Complex That Israel Targeted
item.description: The facility makes materials that Iran says are for civilian uses, but that Israel argues are also deployed in military applications.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of the Mahshahr petrochemical complex in southern Iran in 2011.

item.title: Artisanal or Exploitative? Unraveling the Story of Mexico’s World Cup Jerseys
item.description: Mexicans are enraged after viral videos claimed Adidas exploited Indigenous women to sew World Cup jerseys. We traveled into the mountains to speak to the artisans themselves.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Luis Antonio Rojas
item.media:credit: Antonio Rojas for The New York Times

item.title: No Calm for Tehran’s Residents as Iran-Israel Fighting Resumes
item.description: As explosions sounded in the Iranian capital, people frantically checked phones and social media to see whether they were under attack again.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Ronen Bergman, Jonathan Swan and Max Bearak

item.title: Searching for Shade When It’s 125 Degrees
item.description: Every season brings a new struggle for people in Dadu District, Pakistan, an area prone to sandstorms, drought and flooding.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Asim Hafeez
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: One Is the Pope, the Other an Atheist. They Both Oppose Trump.
item.description: Pope Leo XIV and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain, who met on Monday, have recently clashed with President Trump. Their motivations, however, may be different.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vatican Media, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV welcoming Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain at the Vatican last month, in a photo made available by Vatican media.

item.title: At Least 32 Dead After Earthquake and Landslide in the Philippines
item.description: A 7.8-magnitude quake struck off the island of Mindanao, causing widespread destruction and a deadly landslide. More than 100 others were injured and tens of thousands displaced.
item.dc:creator: Jason Gutierrez, Aie Balagtas See and Sui-Lee Wee
item.media:description: A partially collapsed shopping plaza in General Santos on Monday. “We don’t have a complete picture of the damage, but there are plenty of structures that are cracked,” a civil defense official in the city said.
